entity.name: Yashwanth Kotha
entity.description: Independent developer and designer. Maintainer of AirFloor, a free and open-source air-health tool built for India.

projects.name: AirFloor
projects.description: AirFloor scores every floor of an apartment building in India for how clean and healthy the air is to breathe. It combines live air-quality data (Copernicus CAMS), real ground elevation (Copernicus DEM) and live meteorology (boundary-layer depth, wind at height, atmospheric stability) to model how pollution changes with height, then publishes an honest 0 to 100 score for each floor along with an explicit uncertainty band. Air pollution is one of the largest public-health burdens in India, and the air a family breathes differs meaningfully between the ground floor and the twentieth. That information is normally invisible when people rent or buy a home. AirFloor makes it free, private and available in English, Telugu and Kannada. It is a static site that collects nothing: no accounts, no cookies, no analytics, no backend. Funding is sought to keep it free of ads and paywalls, to cover hosting, a domain and monitoring, and to fund continued maintenance: bias-correcting the model against Indian ground-reference monitors, expanding language coverage, and improving accessibility.

channels.description: No public payment provider is configured yet. Grant disbursement or a bank transfer can be arranged directly with the maintainer by email.

plans.name: One year of maintenance and infrastructure
plans.description: Covers hosting, domain, uptime and error monitoring, and the maintainer's time for one year: bias-correcting the vertical model against Indian reference monitors, widening language coverage beyond English, Telugu and Kannada, and keeping AirFloor free and ad-free for everyone in India.

plans.name: Any amount
plans.description: Any contribution towards keeping AirFloor free, independent and ad-free.
